issue_title: Make voice pipeline an optional Cargo feature (gated subsystem)
issue_body:
## Background

The voice subsystem is currently **unconditionally compiled into `genie-core`** — every binary built from this repo carries the wake-word loop, push-to-talk loop, Whisper/Piper subprocess machinery, AEC, VAD, DSP, identity, intent, language, streaming, format, and pipeline modules. That's roughly **~6,000 LoC across 12 submodules plus `voice_loop.rs`**:

```
crates/genie-core/src/voice/aec.rs        402 lines
crates/genie-core/src/voice/dsp.rs        238
crates/genie-core/src/voice/format.rs     301
crates/genie-core/src/voice/identity.rs   836
crates/genie-core/src/voice/intent.rs     221
crates/genie-core/src/voice/language.rs   190
crates/genie-core/src/voice/mod.rs        333
crates/genie-core/src/voice/noise.rs      355
crates/genie-core/src/voice/pipeline.rs   257
crates/genie-core/src/voice/streaming.rs  158
crates/genie-core/src/voice/stt.rs        959
crates/genie-core/src/voice/tts.rs        493
crates/genie-core/src/voice/vad.rs        120
crates/genie-core/src/voice_loop.rs      1149
                                         -----
                                        ~6,012
```

That code is paid for whether or not it's used. Runtime selection (`voice_enabled` config / `--voice` CLI flag / `GENIEPOD_VOICE=1` env) controls whether the loop *runs*, not whether it *exists in the binary*.

Three real deployment shapes that would benefit from a compile-time gate:

1. **Chat-only Telegram / Slack / Discord deployments.** No microphone, no speakers. Currently ship dead code.
2. **Cross-platform dev (macOS, Windows, non-Jetson Linux).** The voice loop shells out to `arecord` / `aplay` (ALSA, Linux-only) — on other platforms the code compiles but every voice path errors at runtime. A compile-time gate makes the platform-incompatibility explicit at build time.
3. **CI iteration speed.** Workflows from #34 (`ci.yml`, `cross.yml`) currently compile the voice subsystem on every PR even when nothing voice-adjacent changed. A `--no-default-features` build path in the CI matrix would also catch dead-code rot in the voice gate itself.

## Goal

Make the voice pipeline a **Cargo feature** in `genie-core`, **on by default**, that consumers can opt out of with `--no-default-features` or by re-enabling other features explicitly.

`voice_enabled` config / `--voice` CLI / `GENIEPOD_VOICE=1` env continue to work for *runtime* selection on builds that included the feature — those are runtime toggles, this is the compile-time gate.

## Proposed Cargo feature

In `crates/genie-core/Cargo.toml`:

```toml
[features]
default = ["voice"]
voice = []
```

In `crates/genie-core/src/lib.rs`:

```rust
#[cfg(feature = "voice")]
pub mod voice;

#[cfg(feature = "voice")]
pub mod voice_loop;
```

In `crates/genie-core/src/main.rs` — the voice-mode branch (currently `main.rs:170-216`) becomes:

```rust
#[cfg(feature = "voice")]
let voice_mode = std::env::args().any(|a| a == "--voice")
    || std::env::var("GENIEPOD_VOICE").unwrap_or_default() == "1"
    || config.core.voice_enabled;

#[cfg(not(feature = "voice"))]
let voice_mode = {
    // Warn if voice was requested on a build that doesn't include it.
    let requested = std::env::args().any(|a| a == "--voice")
        || std::env::var("GENIEPOD_VOICE").unwrap_or_default() == "1"
        || config.core.voice_enabled;
    if requested {
        tracing::warn!(
            "voice requested via CLI/env/config but this build has \
             --no-default-features (or 'voice' feature is off); ignoring"
        );
    }
    false
};
```

The voice arm of the runtime-mode `if/else` block is also gated:

```rust
if voice_mode {
    #[cfg(feature = "voice")]
    { /* existing voice_loop::run(...) call */ }
    #[cfg(not(feature = "voice"))]
    unreachable!("voice_mode is always false when feature is off");
} else if interactive { ... } else { ... }
```

## What gets gated

**Code that lives behind `#[cfg(feature = "voice")]`:**

- `crates/genie-core/src/voice/` entire module
- `crates/genie-core/src/voice_loop.rs`
- The voice-mode dispatch arm in `main.rs`
- Any voice-related re-exports from `lib.rs`

**Config fields that stay (always parsed, ignored on non-voice builds):**

- `CoreConfig::voice_enabled`
- `CoreConfig::whisper_model`, `whisper_port`, `whisper_cli_path`, `stt_language`
- `CoreConfig::piper_model`, `piper_path`, `piper_pipe_mode`, `voice_tts_models`
- `CoreConfig::audio_device`, `audio_output_device`, `audio_sample_rate`, `audio_denoiser`
- `CoreConfig::deep_filter_path`, `deep_filter_atten_lim_db`, `post_tts_silence_ms`
- `CoreConfig::voice_record_secs`, `voice_continuous`, `voice_continuous_secs`
- `CoreConfig::llm_model_path` (used by voice for GPU time-sharing; also referenced by governor mode-swap logic — keep)
- `CoreConfig::wakeword_script`, `speaker_identity`

These stay in `CoreConfig` so the same `geniepod.toml` can be deployed against a voice-enabled or chat-only build without re-templating. The fields are unused on the chat-only build but don't cause parse errors.

**Systemd units that should be marked optional in the deploy pipeline:**

- `deploy/systemd/genie-wakeword.service`
- `deploy/systemd/genie-whisper.service`
- `deploy/systemd/genie-whisper-warmup.service`
- `deploy/systemd/genie-audio.service`

These can stay in the repo. `make deploy` should grow a `WITH_VOICE=0` knob (or similar) that skips installing the voice-side units. Separate follow-up issue if needed; not in scope for this issue.

## Build commands after this lands

```bash
# Voice-enabled (current default, no change)
cargo build --release -p genie-core
make jetson                       # also unchanged

# Chat-only build (new)
cargo build --release -p genie-core --no-default-features
make jetson FEATURES=""           # or whatever knob is added

# Voice-only (no other future features) — equivalent to default today
cargo build --release -p genie-core --no-default-features --features voice
```

## CI implications

This couples with #34's CI work. The `ci.yml` `test` job should grow a matrix axis:

```yaml
strategy:
  matrix:
    features: ["voice", ""]
```

Without that, the `--no-default-features` build can bit-rot silently — every `#[cfg(feature = "voice")]` gate that misses a transitive reference becomes a compile error only in the unused matrix cell. Once #34 PR-1 lands (#37 with the body rewrite, or a follow-up), adding the feature axis is one YAML line.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `crates/genie-core/Cargo.toml` declares `voice` feature, default-on.
- [ ] `cargo build -p genie-core` (voice on) produces the same binary it does today (size, behavior).
- [ ] `cargo build -p genie-core --no-default-features` builds clean, produces a chat-only binary.
- [ ] `cargo test -p genie-core --no-default-features` runs the non-voice test subset (or skips voice tests cleanly via `#[cfg(feature = "voice")]` on `#[test]` functions).
- [ ] `geniepod.toml` with full voice config block deploys cleanly against a chat-only build and emits one `tracing::warn!` line at startup if `voice_enabled = true` is set but the feature is off.
- [ ] `cargo build --release -p genie-core --no-default-features` on macOS x86_64 succeeds (today the voice ALSA shell-outs are present but unreachable; with the gate, the platform-incompatible code isn't built at all).
- [ ] `genie-ctl` continues to work against both builds — the `/api/health` `llm_backend` and `llm` fields (#39, #40) don't depend on voice.
- [ ] CI matrix (when #34 lands) exercises both `--features voice` and `--no-default-features`.
- [ ] Documentation: a one-paragraph "Build without voice" section in `README.md` or `GETTING_STARTED.md` showing the cargo flag and noting it doesn't change runtime config shape.

## Non-goals (intentionally deferred)

- **No splitting voice into a separate crate.** That's a larger refactor and a different shape of decision; this issue is the feature-flag, not the crate-split. If `genie-voice` ever becomes a goal, it would be a follow-up.
- **No new runtime selector.** `voice_enabled` / `--voice` / `GENIEPOD_VOICE=1` continue to work exactly as today.
- **No change to the default.** Voice stays on by default. This issue is about giving chat-only deployments an opt-out, not flipping the project's primary product positioning.
- **No deploy-pipeline changes** (`make deploy`, `setup-jetson.sh`, systemd unit gating). Track separately if the deploy story needs to grow a `WITH_VOICE=0` knob.

## Alternative interpretations considered

For the record, two other readings of "make voice optional" exist:

**Alt 1: Flip the runtime default to `voice_enabled = false`** and require explicit opt-in.
- Pros: smaller config surface for chat-only by default; matches "optional" more literally.
- Cons: contradicts the product positioning (`README.md` L4: *"A private, always-on AI for your home … Voice in, voice out"*). Existing Jetson deployments would silently lose voice on upgrade. Rejected.

**Alt 2: Extract voice into a separate `genie-voice` crate.**
- Pros: cleanest architectural separation; could be developed/tested independently.
- Cons: substantial refactor (cross-crate references for memory/tools/conversation, new public API surface). The compile-time feature flag in this proposal gets most of the same benefits at a fraction of the cost. Could be a follow-up if `genie-voice` ever becomes a goal in its own right.

The feature flag is the right first move.

## Why now

- Four `genie-ai-runtime` backend slices have landed (#35 / #38 / #39 / #40). The next slice is `[services.llm].backend` config parsing. Once that's in, `genie-claw` will support multiple LLM backends including remote ones — and a remote-LLM deployment with no microphone is a real shape that should produce a minimal binary.
- The macOS dev story (cf. the recent debugging session on the Mac) currently relies on the voice code being compile-clean but runtime-skipped. A feature gate makes the platform contract honest.
- CI is being added (#34). Adding a feature axis to the matrix at workflow-creation time is cheaper than retrofitting later.

## Related

- #34 (CI pipeline) — should add `--no-default-features` to the test matrix once this lands.
- #32 (LLM backend abstraction) — same shape of refactor (an axis of optionality that the codebase already gestures at, codified into a real gate).
